requirements.title: Executive summary present
requirements.description: PSUR must contain an executive summary per MDCG 2022-21 Annex I.

requirements.title: Benefit-risk conclusion statement
requirements.description: Executive summary must include a clear and bold statement on whether the benefit-risk profile has been adversely impacted or remains unchanged.

requirements.title: Previous PSUR actions status
requirements.description: Executive summary must describe the status of actions taken based on the previous PSUR.

requirements.title: Notified Body actions status
requirements.description: Description of actions taken by the Notified Body from review of the previous PSUR.

requirements.title: Data collection period stated
requirements.description: Executive summary must state the data collection (surveillance) period.

requirements.title: Period change justification
requirements.description: If the data collection period changed from the previous PSUR, provide justification and a statement on comparability.

requirements.title: Main results summarised
requirements.description: Executive summary should summarise the main results from the current PSUR.

requirements.title: Device identification
requirements.description: Device trade name(s), Basic UDI-DI(s), EMDN code, and classification rule must be present.

requirements.title: Intended purpose stated
requirements.description: Intended purpose per IFU including indications, contra-indications, and target populations.

requirements.title: Device status declared
requirements.description: Status of the device: on market, no longer placed on market, recalled, or FSCA initiated.

requirements.title: Grouping justification
requirements.description: If multiple devices are grouped in one PSUR, the grouping must be justified with a leading device defined.

requirements.title: CE marking / certification date
requirements.description: Date of first CE marking, declaration of conformity, or EU certificate must be stated.

requirements.title: Volume of sales data present
requirements.description: Sales or distribution volume data must be provided as the denominator for rate calculations.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Art 86.1, Annex II Table 1

requirements.title: Counting method stated
requirements.description: The counting method (units distributed, units shipped, procedures, etc.) must be explicitly stated and consistent.

requirements.title: Year-to-year comparison
requirements.description: Sales data must be presented year-to-year to enable trend detection per Annex II Table 1.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex II Table 1, Annex III

requirements.title: Regional breakdown
requirements.description: Data should be split by region: EEA+TR+XI and Worldwide per Annex III.

requirements.title: Population exposure estimated
requirements.description: Estimated patient exposure independent of sales volume where applicable.

requirements.title: Denominator consistency
requirements.description: The same denominator type must be used consistently throughout all PSUR sections.

requirements.title: Serious incidents characterised
requirements.description: Serious incidents must be characterised from three perspectives: device problem, root cause, and health effect.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Art 87, Annex III

requirements.title: IMDRF coding used
requirements.description: Incidents must use IMDRF AET terminology: Annex A (device problem), Annex C (investigation findings), Annex F (health impact).

requirements.title: Incident rates provided
requirements.description: Both absolute figures and rates of serious incidents must be reported.

requirements.title: Customer complaints summarised
requirements.description: All non-serious complaints and user feedback must be presented.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Annex III, Annex XIV Part B

requirements.title: Complaint grouping with IMDRF codes
requirements.description: Complaints grouped by IMDRF AET Annex A codes (device problem) with both codes and terms.

requirements.title: Occurrence rates reported
requirements.description: Complaint occurrence rates with reference denominator.

requirements.title: CAPA linkage from complaints
requirements.description: Information on whether presented complaints led to initiation of CAPA.

requirements.title: Exclusion justification
requirements.description: Justification for inclusion and exclusion of complaint groups.

requirements.title: Complaint data with IMDRF Annex A
requirements.description: Comprehensive complaint data grouped by IMDRF Annex A codes.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I, Annex II Tables 4-6

requirements.title: Complaint rates vs RACT thresholds
requirements.description: Actual complaint rates compared against maximum expected rates from the RACT.

requirements.title: Year-to-year complaint trending
requirements.description: Year-to-year comparison of complaint rates enabling trend identification.

requirements.title: Regional complaint data
requirements.description: Complaint data split by region (EEA/Worldwide) where applicable.

requirements.title: All IMDRF categories accounted
requirements.description: All IMDRF categories must be addressed, not just the top categories.

requirements.title: Trend monitoring methodology described
requirements.description: Description of statistical trend monitoring methodology (SPC, UCL/LCL).

requirements.title: UCL/LCL control limits defined
requirements.description: Upper and Lower Control Limits clearly stated with calculation basis.

requirements.title: UCL breach investigation
requirements.description: Any UCL breaches must be identified and investigated.

requirements.title: Comparison with previous period
requirements.description: Comparison of trend findings with the previous reporting period.

requirements.title: Trend reports to authorities
requirements.description: Any formal trend reports submitted to regulatory authorities during the period.

requirements.title: Benefit-risk linkage from trends
requirements.description: Trend analysis linked to the overall benefit-risk determination.

requirements.title: FSCA summary present
requirements.description: Summary of all Field Safety Corrective Actions per Annex II Table 7.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Art 87, Annex II Table 7

requirements.title: FSCA details complete
requirements.description: Each FSCA must include: type, date, scope, status, ref number, rationale, impacted regions.

requirements.title: CAPA listing present
requirements.description: All relevant CAPAs listed with type, date, scope, status, ref, description, root cause, effectiveness.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Art 83(4), Annex II Table 8

requirements.title: CAPA effectiveness documented
requirements.description: Closed CAPAs must have effectiveness documented (resolved/not resolved).

requirements.title: Literature search described
requirements.description: Systematic search strategy, databases searched, and inclusion/exclusion criteria.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Annex III, Annex XIV Part B

requirements.title: Findings assessed for safety impact
requirements.description: Literature findings assessed for relevance to device safety and performance.

requirements.title: Comparison with similar devices
requirements.description: Performance and safety compared with similar devices with the same intended purpose.

requirements.title: External databases reviewed
requirements.description: Active surveillance of external regulatory databases (MAUDE, BfArM, etc.).
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Annex III, Annex XIV Part B

requirements.title: Findings from similar devices
requirements.description: Publicly available information from other manufacturers of similar devices.

requirements.title: New risks from external data
requirements.description: Any new risks identified from external data sources must be discussed.

requirements.title: PMCF activities summarised
requirements.description: Summary of PMCF activities and findings per Annex XIV Part B.
requirements.guidance_ref: MDCG 2022-21 Annex I — Art 86, Annex XIV Part B

requirements.title: Overall benefit-risk determination
requirements.description: Final benefit-risk conclusion synthesising all data from all prior sections.

requirements.title: Data limitations acknowledged
requirements.description: Identify limitations to collected data and whether they impact conclusions.

requirements.title: New/emerging risks identified
requirements.description: Outline any new or emerging risks identified during the reporting period.

requirements.title: Actions described
requirements.description: Specific actions taken to address newly identified risks or poor performance.

requirements.title: Manufacturer identification
requirements.description: Manufacturer name, address, and SRN present on cover page or Section B.

requirements.title: PSUR reference number
requirements.description: Unique PSUR reference number assigned and present.

requirements.title: IMDRF terminology used throughout
requirements.description: IMDRF Adverse Event Terminology used consistently across relevant sections.

requirements.title: Stand-alone document
requirements.description: PSUR can be assessed independently from supporting documentation (per MDCG guidance).

requirements.title: Absent data justified
requirements.description: If specific datasets are excluded, the manufacturer justifies the absence.

requirements.title: Consistent denominators
requirements.description: Same denominator used across all sections for rate calculations.

requirements.title: Data compared across sources
requirements.description: Findings from all datasets compared to identify conflicting results.

requirements.title: Table formats per Annex II
requirements.description: Data tables follow the Annex II format guidance (Tables 1–8).

requirements.title: No prohibited regulation citations in narratives
requirements.description: Narratives should not cite specific MDR Article X(Y) or MDCG XXXX patterns — compliance is embedded in the template structure.

requirements.title: No bullet points in narratives
requirements.description: Narratives should use flowing prose without bullet points.

requirements.title: Revision and version tracking
requirements.description: PSUR version number and revision history present.

requirements.title: FSCA-CAPA cross-reference
requirements.description: FSCAs in Section H should cross-reference related CAPAs in Section I.

requirements.title: Trend-incident linkage
requirements.description: Trend findings in Section G should reference incidents from Section D.

requirements.title: Conclusion supported by sections
requirements.description: Section M conclusions must be supported by evidence from all prior sections.

requirements.title: PMS system maintained
requirements.description: Manufacturer must establish and maintain a post-market surveillance system that is proportionate to the risk class and appropriate to the type of device.

requirements.title: PMS data analysis throughout lifetime
requirements.description: The PMS system must include systematic collection and analysis of relevant data throughout the entire lifetime of the device.

requirements.title: PMS plan documented
requirements.description: A PMS plan must be documented in a clear, organised and searchable format. It must specify the device lifetime and include processes for collecting and analysing incidents, complaints, feedback and trends.

requirements.title: PMS plan threshold values
requirements.description: The PMS plan must define threshold values and indicators for risk reassessment including re-evaluation of benefit-risk determination.

requirements.title: PMS plan communication processes
requirements.description: The PMS plan must include effective communication processes between the manufacturer, UK Responsible Person, approved body and Secretary of State.

requirements.title: PMS plan available within 3 working days
requirements.description: The PMS plan must be provided to the Secretary of State within 3 working days of a request.

requirements.title: CAPA when risk identified
requirements.description: The manufacturer must take necessary preventive and corrective action as soon as possible when a risk is identified or non-conformity is suspected, including field safety corrective actions where appropriate.

requirements.title: CAPA notification to UK RP and approved body
requirements.description: The manufacturer must notify the UK Responsible Person, approved body and Secretary of State when undertaking field safety corrective actions.

requirements.title: Serious incident reporting to Secretary of State
requirements.description: Serious incidents must be reported to the Secretary of State. Default timeline is 15 days; 2 days for serious public health threats; 10 days for death or unanticipated serious deterioration in state of health.

requirements.title: Serious incident report content — manufacturer details
requirements.description: Initial serious incident report must include manufacturer details and UK Responsible Person details.

requirements.title: Serious incident report content — device and incident
requirements.description: Initial serious incident report must include device description, incident description, preliminary conclusions and whether FSCA is being considered.

requirements.title: Incident investigation and risk analysis review
requirements.description: The manufacturer must investigate each serious incident, review risk analysis in light of findings and submit a final report detailing investigation methods and conclusions.

requirements.title: Final report — similar incidents and FSCA
requirements.description: The final incident report must include consideration of FSCA, details of any similar incidents and cooperation with the Secretary of State investigation.

requirements.title: FSCA risk assessment and initial report
requirements.description: Before undertaking a field safety corrective action the manufacturer must produce a risk assessment and submit an initial report together with the proposed field safety notice to the Secretary of State.

requirements.title: Field safety notice content
requirements.description: The field safety notice must identify affected devices with UDI, explain the reasons for the FSCA and describe actions to be taken by users.

requirements.title: FSCA final report with effectiveness evidence
requirements.description: A final report on the FSCA must be submitted to the Secretary of State including the outcome and evidence of effectiveness of the corrective action.

requirements.title: FSCA outside GB reported
requirements.description: Any field safety corrective action taken outside Great Britain for the same device model must be reported to the Secretary of State, including a justification if the same FSCA is not being taken in GB.

requirements.title: PMSR for Class I / IVD Class A-B
requirements.description: For Class I and IVD Class A-B devices a post-market surveillance report must be produced within 3 years of placing the device on the UK market and updated at least every 3 years. It must include a summary of PMS results and conclusions and a description of preventive and corrective actions taken.

requirements.title: UK PSUR required for higher-risk classes
requirements.description: A periodic safety update report is required for Class IIa, IIb, III and IVD Class C-D devices. The first PSUR must be produced within 1 year of placing the device on the UK market (2 years for Class IIa) and updated annually (biennially for Class IIa).

requirements.title: UK PSUR content — market and population data
requirements.description: The UK PSUR must include the number of devices placed on the UK market, population characteristics, estimates of population size in the UK and outside the UK and usage frequency.

requirements.title: UK PSUR content — PMS results and risk analysis
requirements.description: The UK PSUR must include a summary of PMS results and conclusions, preventive and corrective actions taken, risk analysis outcomes and PMCF conclusions.

requirements.title: UK PSUR submitted to approved body
requirements.description: The PSUR must be submitted to the approved body responsible for the device.

requirements.title: Trend reporting — statistically significant increase
requirements.description: Any statistically significant increase in frequency or severity of incidents that could adversely impact the risk analysis must be reported to the Secretary of State. Statistical methodology must align with the PMS plan.

requirements.title: Trend reporting — IVD erroneous results
requirements.description: For IVD devices any statistically significant increase in expected erroneous results must also be reported and investigated with a final report submitted.

requirements.title: PMS documentation retention period
requirements.description: All PMS documentation must be retained for the PMS period or 15 years for implantable devices and 10 years for all other devices, whichever is longer.

requirements.title: Documentation available within 3 working days
requirements.description: All PMS documentation must be provided to the Secretary of State within 3 working days of a request.

requirements.title: UK Responsible Person identified
requirements.description: If the device is marketed in the UK the UK Responsible Person must be clearly stated in the PSUR scope section.

requirements.title: UKCA marking status
requirements.description: UKCA marking or UK conformity assessment status must be referenced for devices placed on the GB market.

requirements.title: UK complaint data identifiable
requirements.description: Complaints originating from the UK market must be separately identifiable within the PSUR complaint analysis.

requirements.title: UK customer feedback captured
requirements.description: Customer feedback from UK users must be captured as part of the PMS system and reflected in the PSUR.
